type_definitions.name: TrackStatusOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by usecase operations that derive and expose track status. Replaces direct cli→domain calls to derive_track_status so that the CLI need not import domain::TrackStatus or domain::derive_track_status. The string representation (e.g. 'planned', 'in_progress', 'done') is stable across domain enum changes.

type_definitions.name: GuardDecision
type_definitions.description: Usecase-layer enum mirroring domain::Decision (Allow/Block) without exposing the domain type in the usecase public API. CLI consumes this enum instead of domain::Decision directly, satisfying CN-01.

type_definitions.name: GuardCheckOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by the guard check service. Wraps GuardDecision and the reason string so that the CLI never imports domain::Decision or domain::GuardVerdict directly. The decision field uses the usecase-owned GuardDecision enum.

type_definitions.name: GuardCheckService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the guard shell-command check use case. Driven by the CLI layer. The implementation (GuardCheckInteractor) delegates to the domain guard policy via the ShellParser secondary port. Exposes only usecase-owned types (GuardCheckOutput) in its public API so CLI never imports domain::Decision or domain::GuardVerdict.

type_definitions.name: GuardCheckInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing GuardCheckService. Uses a ShellParserPort secondary port (Arc<dyn ShellParserPort>) for shell command parsing. A usecase-internal ShellParserPortAdapter wraps the ShellParserPort and implements domain::guard::ShellParser by delegation, so the domain guard policy can be called inside the interactor without the CLI importing domain::guard::ShellParser. The domain::GuardVerdict + domain::Decision result is converted into GuardCheckOutput before returning to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait, not the struct fields.

type_definitions.name: ShellParserPort
type_definitions.description: Usecase-owned secondary port (driven port) trait for shell command parsing. Mirrors domain::guard::ShellParser (same split_shell method signature using primitive String types for the parsed commands). CLI composition root injects ConchShellParser (infrastructure) into GuardCheckInteractor without importing domain::guard::ShellParser directly. Inside the usecase layer, a ShellParserPortAdapter struct implements domain::guard::ShellParser by delegating to a ShellParserPort reference, bridging the usecase port to the domain port; this adapter is internal to the usecase crate and invisible to the CLI.

type_definitions.name: SchemaExporterPort
type_definitions.description: Usecase-owned secondary port (driven port) trait for schema export. The single method accepts a crate name and returns the serialized JSON schema string, using only primitive types so the CLI never imports domain::schema::SchemaExporter or domain::schema::SchemaExport directly. Infrastructure (RustdocSchemaExporter) implements both domain::schema::SchemaExporter and usecase::SchemaExporterPort; its SchemaExporterPort impl calls the domain export method internally and serializes the result before returning. CLI composition root injects RustdocSchemaExporter into ExportSchemaInteractor as Arc<dyn SchemaExporterPort> without importing the domain trait.

type_definitions.name: ExportSchemaService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the domain schema export use case (sotp domain export-schema). Driven by the CLI layer. Wraps domain::schema::SchemaExporter so the CLI never imports the domain port trait directly. Returns the serialized JSON schema as a String rather than exposing internal domain types.

type_definitions.name: ExportSchemaCommand
type_definitions.description: CQRS command object for the schema export use case. Carries the crate name to export. Owned by usecase so the CLI does not import domain::schema::SchemaExporter.

type_definitions.name: ExportSchemaError
type_definitions.description: Error type for the ExportSchemaService. Wraps failures from the rustdoc schema export and JSON serialization steps without leaking domain::schema::SchemaExportError directly across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: ExportSchemaInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing ExportSchemaService. Uses a SchemaExporterPort secondary port (Arc<dyn SchemaExporterPort>) for schema export. Calls SchemaExporterPort::export_as_json, which returns an already-serialized JSON string, so the interactor never imports domain::schema::SchemaExporter or domain::schema::SchemaExport directly. Returns ExportSchemaError on failure. The CLI sees only ExportSchemaService and ExportSchemaCommand; domain::schema::SchemaExporter is an implementation detail of the infrastructure adapter. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: TaskOperationOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by track task operations (transition, add-task, set-override, clear-override). Contains the derived track status string so the CLI can print status without calling domain::derive_track_status directly. The task_id field is present for add-task; optional for others.

type_definitions.name: ReviewApprovalOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by the review check-approved operation. Carries decision (Approved/ApprovedWithBypass/Blocked), optional bypass scope count, and the list of required scopes when blocked. Replaces direct cli→domain::review_v2::ReviewApprovalVerdict usage so the CLI never imports domain review types directly.

type_definitions.name: ReviewApprovalDecision
type_definitions.description: Enum representing the possible review approval outcomes exposed by the usecase boundary. Mirrors domain::review_v2::ReviewApprovalVerdict without exposing the domain type. Used by ReviewApprovalOutput.

type_definitions.name: ReviewCheckApprovedService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the review check-approved use case. Driven by the CLI. Takes string track_id and items_dir to avoid importing domain::TrackId in the CLI. Returns ReviewApprovalOutput which is a usecase-owned DTO.

type_definitions.name: ReviewCheckApprovedError
type_definitions.description: Error type for ReviewCheckApprovedService. Wraps invalid track ID, review store failures, and approval evaluation failures without leaking domain error types across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: ReviewCheckApprovedInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing ReviewCheckApprovedService. Constructs domain types internally and converts results to ReviewApprovalOutput before returning to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: HookDispatchCommand
type_definitions.description: CQRS command object for the hook dispatch use case. Carries the raw tool_name, optional command string, optional file_path, and optional content string from the Claude Code hook JSON envelope. The CLI parses the envelope into this command so that the hook dispatcher (usecase) never requires domain::hook::HookInput to be constructed at the CLI boundary.

type_definitions.name: HookVerdictOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by the hook dispatch service to the CLI. Wraps the verdict outcome (Allow/Block) and a reason string without exposing domain::hook::HookVerdict across the usecase boundary. The CLI maps HookVerdictDecision::Block to exit 2, Allow to exit 0.

type_definitions.name: HookVerdictDecision
type_definitions.description: Enum representing the hook verdict outcome at the usecase boundary. Mirrors domain::hook::HookVerdict (Allow/Block) without exposing the domain type. Used by HookVerdictOutput.

type_definitions.name: HookDispatchService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the hook dispatch use case (sotp hook dispatch). Driven by the CLI layer. Takes a HookDispatchCommand (raw CLI input) and a hook name string, dispatches to the appropriate HookHandler, and returns HookVerdictOutput so the CLI never imports domain::hook::HookContext, domain::hook::HookInput, or domain::hook::HookVerdict directly.

type_definitions.name: HookDispatchError
type_definitions.description: Error type for HookDispatchService. Wraps unknown hook name and handler execution failures without leaking domain::hook::HookError across the usecase boundary. Fail-closed: any error maps to block (exit 2) at the CLI.

type_definitions.name: HookDispatchInteractor
type_definitions.description: Struct implementing HookDispatchService. Wires GuardHookHandler, TestFileDeletionGuardHandler, and skill compliance handler at construction. Converts HookDispatchCommand into the domain HookInput internally, calls the appropriate handler via usecase::hook::dispatch, and converts the domain HookVerdict into HookVerdictOutput. The CLI never sees domain::hook::* types.

type_definitions.name: ReviewRoundType
type_definitions.description: Usecase-layer enum mirroring domain::RoundType (Fast/Final). Used internally by RunReviewService and its interactor when converting the string round_type field from RunReviewCommand to the domain type. The CLI passes round_type as a plain String in RunReviewCommand so it never needs to import ReviewRoundType or domain::RoundType directly; the interactor converts the string to ReviewRoundType internally.

type_definitions.name: TrackPhaseOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by the track resolve (phase resolution) use case. Wraps the current phase name, reason, recommended next command, and optional blocker string so that the CLI never imports domain::track_phase::TrackPhaseInfo or domain::ImplPlanReader directly.

type_definitions.name: TrackPhaseService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the track phase resolution use case (sotp track resolve). Driven by the CLI layer. Takes string track_id so the CLI does not need to construct domain::TrackId. Returns TrackPhaseOutput which contains all display information for the resolve command.

type_definitions.name: TrackPhaseError
type_definitions.description: Error type for TrackPhaseService. Wraps invalid track ID, missing track, and implementation plan load failures without leaking domain::RepositoryError across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: TrackPhaseInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing TrackPhaseService. Follows the same internal generic-storage pattern as the existing TransitionTaskUseCase: the struct holds a private Arc<S> field where S satisfies domain storage traits (TrackReader + ImplPlanReader) as an implementation detail. CLI composition root wires FsTrackStore as S and injects the result as Arc<dyn TrackPhaseService>, so the generic bound never crosses the usecase→CLI boundary; CLI commands only see the dyn TrackPhaseService trait object (CN-01 satisfied). Constructs domain::TrackId internally, calls domain::track_phase::resolve_phase, and returns TrackPhaseOutput. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueConsistencyService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the verify catalogue-consistency use case (sotp verify catalogue-consistency). Driven by the CLI layer. Wraps domain::check_consistency so the CLI never imports domain::TypeCatalogueDocument, domain::TypeGraph, domain::TypeBaseline, or domain::ConsistencyReport directly.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueConsistencyOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by VerifyCatalogueConsistencyService. Contains the pass/fail verdict and a list of finding strings so the CLI can print results without importing domain::ConsistencyReport or domain::verify::VerifyFinding directly.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueConsistencyError
type_definitions.description: Error type for VerifyCatalogueConsistencyService. Wraps invalid track ID, catalogue decode failures, schema export failures, and baseline load failures without leaking domain error types or domain::schema::SchemaExportError across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueConsistencyInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing VerifyCatalogueConsistencyService. Constructs domain types internally and converts results to VerifyCatalogueConsistencyOutput before returning to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueSpecSignalsService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the verify catalogue-spec-signals use case (sotp verify catalogue-spec-signals). Driven by the CLI layer. Wraps domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals so the CLI never imports domain::CatalogueSpecSignal, domain::evaluate_catalogue_entry_signal, or domain::ContentHash directly.

type_definitions.name: VerifySpecSignalsOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by VerifyCatalogueSpecSignalsService. Contains the pass/fail verdict and a list of finding strings for each layer, so the CLI can print results without importing domain signal types directly.

type_definitions.name: VerifySpecSignalsError
type_definitions.description: Error type for VerifyCatalogueSpecSignalsService. Wraps track ID validation, catalogue decode, and spec signal evaluation failures without leaking domain::ConfidenceSignal or domain::ContentHash across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueSpecSignalsInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing VerifyCatalogueSpecSignalsService. Constructs domain types internally and converts results to VerifySpecSignalsOutput before returning to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: TypeSignalsService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the track type-signals use case (sotp track type-signals). Driven by the CLI layer. Wraps domain::schema::SchemaExporter and domain::TypeSignalsDocument so the CLI never imports these domain types directly. Evaluates type signals for each TDDD-enabled layer catalogue and writes the updated signal document.

type_definitions.name: LayerSignalSummary
type_definitions.description: DTO returned per layer by TypeSignalsService. Contains the layer_id, blue count, yellow count, and red count so the CLI can print signal summaries without importing domain::ConfidenceSignal or domain::TypeSignalsDocument directly.

type_definitions.name: TypeSignalsError
type_definitions.description: Error type for TypeSignalsService. Wraps invalid track ID, inactive track (done/archived), unknown layer, catalogue decode failures, and schema export failures without leaking domain::schema::SchemaExportError or domain::TrackStatus across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: TypeSignalsInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing TypeSignalsService. Constructs domain types internally and converts results to Vec<LayerSignalSummary> before returning to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: VerifyAdrSignals
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the verify-ADR-signals use case, defined in usecase::verify_adr_signals. This track modifies its verify method signature to return AdrVerifyOutput (a usecase-owned DTO) instead of the domain::AdrVerifyReport, so that CLI commands/verify.rs never imports domain::AdrVerifyReport directly after the T012 migration. VerifyAdrSignalsCommand and VerifyAdrSignalsError remain as the existing usecase-layer types (no change to those types).

type_definitions.name: VerifyAdrSignalsInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing VerifyAdrSignals. This track modifies its impl VerifyAdrSignals::verify to return AdrVerifyOutput (a usecase-owned DTO) instead of domain::AdrVerifyReport, mirroring the signature change declared on the VerifyAdrSignals application service trait. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: AdrVerifyOutput
type_definitions.description: Usecase-owned DTO returned by VerifyAdrSignals::verify. Wraps the four signal-band counts from domain::AdrVerifyReport (blue_count, yellow_count, red_count, grandfathered_count) so that CLI commands/verify.rs can read the counts without importing domain::AdrVerifyReport. The CLI uses red_count >= 1 to decide the non-zero exit code, preserving the existing external behavior per AC-10.

type_definitions.name: ScopeQueryService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for scope query (classify/files) use cases, defined in usecase::review_v2::scope_query. This track modifies it to replace the existing domain-typed methods with string-accepting methods (classify_by_strings, files_by_string) so that CLI review subcommands (classify.rs, files.rs) can pass Vec<String> paths and String scope names without importing domain::review_v2::FilePath, domain::review_v2::ScopeName, or domain::review_v2::MainScopeName. The FilePath and ScopeName construction from strings happens inside the interactor implementation, keeping domain types hidden behind the usecase boundary per CN-01 and AC-03.

type_definitions.name: ScopeClassificationOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by ScopeQueryService::classify_by_strings. Contains the string path and the classification encoded in `scopes`: a Vec<String> where each entry is either a named scope name (for Named classification), 'other' (for the implicit Other scope), or '<excluded>' (for paths filtered by operational/other-track patterns). This single-field encoding avoids a separate classification_kind field while keeping the struct free of domain types (CN-01 / AC-03). CLI uses entry.scopes.join(',') for display.

type_definitions.name: ScopeQueryInteractor
type_definitions.description: Struct implementing ScopeQueryService. This track modifies ScopeQueryInteractor to add the two new string-accepting methods (classify_by_strings, files_by_string) that replace the old domain-typed methods. The interactor constructs domain::review_v2::FilePath and domain::review_v2::ScopeName from the input strings internally, performs the domain classification, and converts results to ScopeClassificationOutput DTOs before returning. The CLI sees only ScopeQueryService and string/primitive types; domain::review_v2::FilePath, domain::review_v2::ScopeName, and domain::review_v2::MainScopeName remain invisible across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: TaskOperationError
type_definitions.description: Error type for track task operation use cases (transition, add-task, set-override, clear-override, next-task, task-counts). Wraps domain::DomainError, domain::TrackReadError, domain::TrackWriteError, domain::RepositoryError, and domain::WorktreeError so that cli/src/error.rs can remove the five #[from] domain error variants from CliError. The CLI converts TaskOperationError to CliError::Message, eliminating the direct domain error coupling at the cli boundary.

type_definitions.name: TaskOperationInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing TaskOperationService. Follows the same internal generic-storage pattern as the existing TransitionTaskUseCase: the struct holds a private Arc<S> field where S satisfies domain storage traits (TrackReader + TrackWriter + ImplPlanReader + ImplPlanWriter) as an implementation detail. CLI composition root wires FsTrackStore as S and injects the result as Arc<dyn TaskOperationService>, so the generic bound never crosses the usecase→CLI boundary; CLI commands only see the dyn TaskOperationService trait object (CN-01 satisfied). Constructs domain::TrackId, domain::TaskId, domain::CommitHash internally. Returns TaskOperationOutput to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: TaskTransitionCommand
type_definitions.description: CQRS command object for the task transition use case. Carries standard-library-typed fields only (String, PathBuf, bool — no domain types): items_dir (PathBuf, file-system root), track_id, task_id, target_status, optional commit_hash, and skip_branch_check. PathBuf is a std library path type, not a domain type. The interactor constructs domain::TrackId, domain::TaskId, domain::CommitHash internally.

type_definitions.name: AddTaskCommand
type_definitions.description: CQRS command object for the add-task use case. Carries standard-library-typed fields only (String, PathBuf — no domain types): items_dir (PathBuf, file-system root), track_id, description, optional section, optional after_task_id. PathBuf is a std library path type, not a domain type. The interactor constructs domain::TrackId and domain::TaskId internally.

type_definitions.name: SetOverrideCommand
type_definitions.description: CQRS command object for the set-override use case. Carries standard-library-typed fields only (String, PathBuf, bool — no domain types): items_dir (PathBuf, file-system root), track_id, status ('blocked' | 'cancelled'), reason, and skip_branch_check. PathBuf is a std library path type, not a domain type. The interactor constructs domain::TrackId and domain::StatusOverride internally.

type_definitions.name: ClearOverrideCommand
type_definitions.description: CQRS command object for the clear-override use case. Carries standard-library-typed fields only (String, PathBuf, bool — no domain types): items_dir (PathBuf, file-system root), track_id, and skip_branch_check. PathBuf is a std library path type, not a domain type. The interactor constructs domain::TrackId internally.

type_definitions.name: TaskOperationService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for track task mutation operations. Driven by the CLI. Accepts command objects (TaskTransitionCommand, AddTaskCommand, SetOverrideCommand, ClearOverrideCommand) that carry only string/primitive fields, so the CLI never imports domain::TrackId, domain::TaskId, domain::CommitHash, domain::StatusOverride, or domain::DomainError directly. Each method returns TaskOperationOutput on success. The interactor implementation constructs domain types internally and persists changes via domain storage trait bounds (TrackReader + TrackWriter + ImplPlanReader + ImplPlanWriter); the CLI composition root injects FsTrackStore (a secondary_adapter that implements those domain traits).

type_definitions.name: TaskQueryService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for next-task and task-counts queries. Driven by the CLI. Accepts string track_id and items_dir so the CLI never imports domain::TrackId, domain::ImplPlanReader, or domain::TaskStatusKind directly. Returns simple serialisable DTOs that the CLI formats for stdout.

type_definitions.name: TaskQueryInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing TaskQueryService. Constructs domain types internally and converts results to NextTaskOutput or TaskCountsOutput before returning to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: NextTaskOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by TaskQueryService::next_task. Contains the task_id and description of the next open task so the CLI can print them without importing domain task types.

type_definitions.name: TaskCountsOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by TaskQueryService::task_counts. Contains per-status counts (todo, in_progress, done, skipped) so the CLI can print counts without importing domain::TaskStatusKind.

type_definitions.name: PreCommitTypeSignalsService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the pre-commit type-signal recomputation use case (sotp make track-commit-message pre-commit step). Driven by the CLI layer. Wraps the ConfidenceSignal classification logic so that commands/make.rs never imports domain::ConfidenceSignal directly. Returns PreCommitTypeSignalsOutput containing the signal verdict and optional layer bindings for downstream catalogue-spec steps.

type_definitions.name: PreCommitTypeSignalsOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by PreCommitTypeSignalsService::run. Contains the overall verdict (pass/blocked), list of red signal names, list of yellow signal names, and whether the track is frozen (Done/Archived). The frozen flag lets the caller skip downstream catalogue-spec steps without re-reading domain state. CLI uses this DTO to produce actionable pre-commit messages without importing domain::ConfidenceSignal.

type_definitions.name: PreCommitTypeSignalsError
type_definitions.description: Error type for PreCommitTypeSignalsService. Wraps git discover failures, architecture-rules.json parse errors, symlink guard rejections, and track metadata load failures without leaking domain error types across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: PreCommitTypeSignalsInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing PreCommitTypeSignalsService. Constructs domain types internally and converts results to PreCommitTypeSignalsOutput before returning to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: RunReviewCommand
type_definitions.description: CQRS command object for the run-review use case (sotp review codex-local). Carries standard-library-typed fields only (String, PathBuf, u64 — no domain types): track_id, round_type ('fast' | 'final' as String), group name, model, timeout_seconds (u64), base_prompt, optional briefing_file (PathBuf), and items_dir (PathBuf, file-system root). PathBuf is a std library path type, not a domain type. The CLI never imports domain::TrackId, domain::RoundType, domain::ReviewGroupName, or the usecase ReviewRoundType enum directly. The interactor converts round_type string to ReviewRoundType internally.

type_definitions.name: RunReviewOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by RunReviewService. Contains verdict_kind ('approved' | 'rejected' | 'skipped'), finding count, and an optional human-readable summary. Replaces direct cli consumption of domain::review_v2::Verdict, domain::review_v2::FastVerdict, and domain::review_v2::ReviewOutcome so the CLI display layer never imports domain review types.

type_definitions.name: RunReviewError
type_definitions.description: Error type for RunReviewService. Wraps invalid track ID, invalid group name, composition root failures, and reviewer execution failures without leaking domain::review_v2::ReviewCycleError or domain review types across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: RunReviewInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing RunReviewService. Builds the ReviewCycle composition root internally from string-typed command fields, executes the review, and converts domain types to RunReviewOutput before returning to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: RunReviewService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the run-review use case (sotp review codex-local). Driven by the CLI layer. Accepts RunReviewCommand containing string-typed fields so the CLI never imports domain::TrackId, domain::RoundType, domain::ReviewGroupName, domain::review_v2::Verdict, domain::review_v2::FastVerdict, or domain::review_v2::ReviewOutcome. The interactor builds the ReviewCycle composition root internally and converts domain types to RunReviewOutput.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for the verify catalogue-spec-refs use case (sotp verify catalogue-spec-refs). Driven by the CLI layer. Encapsulates domain::check_catalogue_spec_ref_integrity, domain::ContentHash, domain::SpecRefFinding, and domain::SpecElementId so that commands/verify_catalogue_spec_refs.rs never imports these domain types directly. Returns a VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsOutput DTO that the CLI formats for stderr.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsOutput
type_definitions.description: DTO returned by VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsService. Contains passed (true = no findings) and a list of pre-formatted finding strings ready for the CLI to emit to stderr, one per line. Pre-formatting in the interactor avoids the CLI needing to pattern-match on SpecRefFindingKind variants.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsError
type_definitions.description: Error type for VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsService. Wraps symlink guard rejections, invalid track ID, missing architecture-rules.json, missing track directory, missing spec.json, catalogue decode failures, and signal decode failures without leaking domain::ContentHash, domain::SpecElementId, or domain::tddd::LayerId across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsService. Constructs domain types internally and converts results to VerifyCatalogueSpecRefsOutput before returning to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: CommitHashPersistenceService
type_definitions.description: Application service trait for persisting the HEAD SHA to .commit_hash after a successful commit (sotp make track-commit-message post-commit step). Driven by the CLI layer. Wraps domain::CommitHash and domain::review_v2::CommitHashWriter so that commands/make.rs never imports these domain types directly.

type_definitions.name: CommitHashPersistenceError
type_definitions.description: Error type for CommitHashPersistenceService. Wraps invalid track ID, git discover failures, branch mismatch, git rev-parse failures, invalid SHA, and store write failures without leaking domain::CommitHash or domain::review_v2::CommitHashWriter across the usecase boundary.

type_definitions.name: CommitHashPersistenceInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing CommitHashPersistenceService. Constructs domain types (CommitHash) internally and invokes CommitHashWriter to persist the HEAD SHA without leaking domain types to the CLI. DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: HookShellParserPort
type_definitions.description: Usecase-owned secondary port (driven port) trait for full-fidelity shell command parsing used by hook dispatch. Returns Result<Vec<SimpleCommand>, ParseError> — preserving redirect_texts and has_output_redirect fields from domain::guard::SimpleCommand — so that hook guard policy can enforce block-direct-git-ops and block-test-file-deletion without security regression. Unlike ShellParserPort (lossy Vec<String>), this port exposes domain::guard::SimpleCommand and domain::guard::ParseError in its signature; HookDispatchInteractor receives the full struct rather than raw strings. The infrastructure adapter ConchShellParser implements this port. CLI composition root injects ConchShellParser as Arc<dyn HookShellParserPort> into the hook dispatch interactor, so cli/hook_dispatch.rs never needs to import domain::guard::ShellParser directly. NOTE: this port is an intentional exception to CN-01 (no domain types in usecase public API) for security reasons — the full SimpleCommand struct is required to enforce redirect-based guard rules; the exception is recorded in ADR D1 subsection 'Security-first port design (hook dispatch CN-01 exception)'.

type_definitions.name: ScopeQueryError
type_definitions.description: Error type for ScopeQueryService operations. The T010-T013 migration added two new variants (InvalidPath, InvalidScopeName) to support the new string-accepting classify_by_strings and files_by_string methods, which construct domain::review_v2::FilePath and domain::review_v2::ScopeName internally and surface parse failures as usecase-layer errors.

type_definitions.name: ActivateTrackUseCase
type_definitions.description: Use case struct for activating a planning-only track (assigning a branch and materializing it). The T010-T013 migration added execute_by_strings, a string-accepting wrapper around execute, so that the CLI (commands/track/activate.rs) no longer needs to construct domain::TrackId or domain::TrackBranch directly (CN-01 / AC-03). NOTE: execute_by_strings returns Result<ActivateTrackOutcome, TrackWriteError> where TrackWriteError is a domain error type — this is an intentional accepted CN-01 exception recorded in spec.json IN-10 (parallel to IN-08/IN-09). CLI callers pattern-match Err(err) without any `use domain::...TrackWriteError` import; cargo make check-layers passes.

informal_grounds.summary: execute_by_strings wraps execute; TrackWriteError re-used for ergonomics. Usecase-owned error type deferred. CLI callers do not import TrackWriteError directly; cargo make check-layers passes.

type_definitions.name: RenderContractMapCommand
type_definitions.description: Command object for RenderContractMap::execute. The T010-T013 migration migrated the fields from domain types (TrackId, LayerId, TypeDefinitionKind) to plain Rust types (String, Vec<String>) so that CLI callers (commands/track/contract_map.rs) never import domain types directly (CN-01 / AC-03). The interactor converts these strings to domain types internally.

type_definitions.name: RenderContractMapError
type_definitions.description: Error type for RenderContractMap::execute. The T010-T013 migration added the InvalidTrackId variant (emitted when the track_id string fails TrackId::try_new validation) to account for the new string-accepting command fields.

type_definitions.name: RenderContractMapOutput
type_definitions.description: Output DTO returned by RenderContractMap::execute on success. Carries lightweight metrics (rendered_layer_count, total_entry_count) so CLI callers can report a post-write summary without re-reading the generated markdown. Pre-existing type; no field changes in this track.

type_definitions.name: RenderContractMap
type_definitions.description: Application service trait (primary port) for the Contract Map render workflow. CLI composition root injects RenderContractMapInteractor and dispatches through this trait so the CLI stays substitutable. This track modifies RenderContractMapCommand fields (String-typed track_id / kind_filter / layer_filter instead of domain TrackId / TypeDefinitionKind / LayerId), so the execute signature indirectly changes: cmd is now &RenderContractMapCommand with String fields. The trait method signature itself is unchanged.

type_definitions.name: RenderContractMapInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing RenderContractMap (primary port). Composes a CatalogueLoader and a ContractMapWriter. This track modifies its execute implementation to accept the updated RenderContractMapCommand (String-typed fields) and convert them to domain types internally (TrackId::try_new, TypeDefinitionKind lookup, LayerId). DI fields are private implementation details; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the service trait.

type_definitions.name: LintRuleKind
type_definitions.description: Usecase-owned mirror enum of domain::tddd::catalogue_linter::CatalogueLinterRuleKind. Allows CLI callers to specify lint rule types (FieldEmpty, FieldNonEmpty, KindLayerConstraint) without importing the domain type directly (CN-01 / AC-03). The interactor converts LintRuleSpec values containing LintRuleKind to domain CatalogueLinterRule internally.

type_definitions.name: LintRuleSpec
type_definitions.description: Usecase-owned string-only description of a single catalogue lint rule. Replaces direct domain::tddd::catalogue_linter::CatalogueLinterRule usage in RunCatalogueLintCommand.rules so that CLI callers (commands/track/lint.rs) never import domain lint types directly (CN-01 / AC-03). The interactor converts each LintRuleSpec to a CatalogueLinterRule internally, failing with RunCatalogueLintError::InvalidRuleSpec on unrecognised values.

informal_grounds.summary: dto placed in usecase layer: LintRuleSpec is a string-only boundary mirror of domain::tddd::catalogue_linter::CatalogueLinterRule; it does not cross a serde/persistence boundary but is required at the usecase boundary so CLI callers never import domain lint types (CN-01 / AC-03).

type_definitions.name: RunCatalogueLintCommand
type_definitions.description: Command input for RunCatalogueLint::execute. The T010-T013 migration changed the rules field from Vec<CatalogueLinterRule> (domain type) to Vec<LintRuleSpec> (usecase-owned DTO) so that CLI callers never import domain lint types directly (CN-01 / AC-03). The interactor converts each LintRuleSpec to a domain CatalogueLinterRule internally.

type_definitions.name: RunCatalogueLintError
type_definitions.description: Error type for RunCatalogueLint::execute. The T010-T013 migration added the InvalidRuleSpec variant (emitted when a LintRuleSpec cannot be converted to a domain CatalogueLinterRule) to account for the new string-based rule input.

type_definitions.name: RunCatalogueLintInteractor
type_definitions.description: Concrete struct implementing RunCatalogueLint. Generic over CatalogueLoader and CatalogueLinter secondary ports. The CLI composition root wires FsCatalogueLoader and DomainCatalogueLinter as concrete types without importing the domain port traits directly. DI fields are private; expected_members is empty because the public type contract is captured by the RunCatalogueLint application service trait.

type_definitions.name: RunCatalogueLint
type_definitions.description: Primary port (application service trait) for running catalogue lint rules against a single layer. Implemented by RunCatalogueLintInteractor. Driven by CLI callers (commands/track/lint.rs) which wire the interactor generics at the composition root. NOTE: execute returns Vec<CatalogueLintViolation> where CatalogueLintViolation is a domain value object — this is an intentional accepted CN-01 exception recorded in spec.json IN-08 (parallel to HookShellParserPort / IN-07). A usecase-owned DTO was deferred; CLI callers access violations via type inference without any `use domain::...CatalogueLintViolation` import, so cargo make check-layers passes.

informal_grounds.summary: CN-01 exception accepted: RunCatalogueLint::execute returns Vec<CatalogueLintViolation> (domain value object). A usecase-owned CatalogueLintViolation DTO was intentionally deferred; CLI callers access the result via type inference without a direct `use domain::...CatalogueLintViolation` import, so cargo make check-layers passes. This is the same pattern as HookShellParserPort (domain SimpleCommand crosses the boundary at the port level).

type_definitions.name: check_compliance_render
type_definitions.description: Top-level pub fn in usecase::skill_compliance. Runs a skill compliance check and returns the rendered additionalContext string if any compliance guidance should be injected, or None otherwise. Wraps domain::skill_compliance::check_compliance so that CLI hook handlers (commands/hook.rs) never import domain skill-compliance types directly (CN-01 / AC-03).

type_definitions.name: has_skill_command
type_definitions.description: Top-level pub fn in usecase::skill_compliance. Returns true when the given prompt contains a recognized /track:* command. Wraps domain::skill_compliance::detect_skill_command for early-exit guards so that CLI hook handlers (commands/hook.rs) never import domain::skill_compliance::SkillMatch or domain::skill_compliance::ComplianceContext directly (CN-01 / AC-03).

type_definitions.name: reject_branchless_guard_by_str
type_definitions.description: Top-level pub fn in usecase::track_resolution. String-based wrapper around reject_branchless_guard for use by the CLI layer. Validates track_id_str via TrackId::try_new internally and delegates to reject_branchless_guard. NOTE: the reader parameter is `&impl TrackReader` (a domain trait) — this is an intentional accepted CN-01 exception recorded in spec.json IN-09 (parallel to IN-08 / HookShellParserPort). CLI callers pass a concrete FsTrackStore by type inference with no `use domain::...TrackReader` import in apps/cli/src/; cargo make check-layers passes. A usecase-owned TrackReader port was deferred.

informal_grounds.summary: CN-01 exception accepted: &impl TrackReader (domain trait) appears in the function signature. A usecase-owned port abstraction was intentionally deferred; CLI callers pass a concrete FsTrackStore via type inference without any `use domain::...TrackReader` import in apps/cli/src/, so cargo make check-layers passes. This pattern parallels HookShellParserPort where domain types cross the boundary at the port level.

type_definitions.name: reject_branchless_implementation_transition_by_str
type_definitions.description: Top-level pub fn in usecase::track_resolution. String-based wrapper around reject_branchless_implementation_transition for use by the CLI layer (CN-01 / AC-03: no domain types in apps/cli/src/). Validates track_id_str via TrackId::try_new internally. The CLI passes a raw string and never imports domain::TrackId directly.
